user.title: gsmnode
user.description: Connect to your gsmnode API Server.

data_description.api_base: For example https://gsmnode.example.com or http://10.2.1.10:8080 — it must be reachable from Home Assistant. Plain http sends your password, your messages and the recipients' numbers in the clear: use https unless the gateway is on a network you trust completely.
data_description.device_id: Device ID of the phone to send through by default. Leave empty to use the most recently seen one.

reconfigure.title: gsmnode
reconfigure.description: Update where the API Server lives, or the login used to reach it.

data_description.api_base: For example https://gsmnode.example.com or http://10.2.1.10:8080 — it must be reachable from Home Assistant. Plain http sends your password, your messages and the recipients' numbers in the clear: use https unless the gateway is on a network you trust completely.

reauth_confirm.title: gsmnode
reauth_confirm.description: The API Server rejected the saved credentials for {email}. Enter the password again.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the API Server. Check the URL and that it is reachable.

abort.account_mismatch: Those settings point at a different gateway account. Add it as a new integration instead.

init.title: gsmnode
init.description:
What would you like to set up?

Notification targets live on the integration's page, under "Add notification target".

menu_options.panel: Sidebar item — show an overview inside Home Assistant
menu_options.events: Incoming events — hear about SMS, calls and delivery reports

panel.title: gsmnode sidebar

data_description.panel_url: Must be reachable from the browser, not from Home Assistant. A page served over http cannot be embedded in a Home Assistant served over https.
data_description.panel_title: The name shown in the sidebar. Give each gateway its own if you have more than one.

events.title: Incoming events

data_description.callback_url: Where the gateway should reach Home Assistant, if that differs from the address Home Assistant knows itself by. Just the base, e.g. http://10.2.1.20:8123 — the webhook path is added for you. Deliveries are signed either way, so a forged one is rejected.

error.url_required: A custom overview needs an address.

options.web_app: Web App — the full gateway UI
options.api_panel: API Server panel — status and administration

options.mms: MMS — text with a subject and attachments

send.name: Send
send.description: Send an SMS or MMS, or place a call, choosing how it goes out.

phone_numbers.name: Phone numbers
phone_numbers.description: One or more recipients. A call is placed to each in turn.

message.name: Message
message.description: The text to send. Required for an SMS, optional on an MMS with an attachment, ignored for a call.

attachments.name: Attachments
attachments.description: Files to send with an MMS, by path. Each must be under an allowed directory and at most 1 MB.

sim_number.name: SIM slot
sim_number.description: Which SIM to send or dial from, counting from 0.

schedule_at.name: Send at
schedule_at.description: Hold the message until this time instead of sending it now.

config_entry_id.name: Gateway
config_entry_id.description: Which gsmnode gateway to send through. Only needed when more than one is configured.

device_id.name: Phone (by gateway id)
device_id.description: Alternative to Phone, naming the gateway's own device ID.

send_sms.name: Send SMS
send_sms.description: Send an SMS through the gateway.

config_entry_id.name: Gateway
config_entry_id.description: Which gsmnode gateway to send through. Only needed when more than one is configured.

phone_numbers.name: Phone numbers
phone_numbers.description: One or more recipient phone numbers.

device_id.name: Phone (by gateway id)
device_id.description: Alternative to Phone, naming the gateway's own device ID.

sim_number.name: SIM slot
sim_number.description: Which SIM to send on, counting from 0. Leave empty for the phone's default SIM.

schedule_at.name: Send at
schedule_at.description: Hold the message until this time instead of sending it now.

call.name: Place call
call.description: Tell a gateway phone to place an outbound call.

config_entry_id.name: Gateway
config_entry_id.description: Which gsmnode gateway to call through. Only needed when more than one is configured.

device_id.name: Phone (by gateway id)
device_id.description: Alternative to Phone, naming the gateway's own device ID.

sim_number.name: SIM slot
sim_number.description: Which SIM to dial from, counting from 0.

no_gateway.message: No gsmnode gateway is set up. Add the integration first.

ambiguous_gateway.message: More than one gsmnode gateway is set up. Say which one to use in the Gateway field.

entry_not_loaded.message: The gsmnode gateway {entry_id} is unknown or not loaded.

auth_failed.message: The API Server rejected the gsmnode credentials: {error}

request_failed.message: The gsmnode API Server did not accept the request: {error}

mms_empty.message: An MMS needs a message or at least one attachment.

path_not_allowed.message: {path} is not in an allowed directory. Add its folder to allowlist_external_dirs.

attachment_too_big.message: {path} is larger than {limit} kB, too big to send as an MMS.

user.title: Add notification target
user.description:
A target becomes its own notify entity, so everything it cannot be told per message is decided here: what to send, to whom, and from which phone.

The SIM comes next — the phones report their own, so you pick from the list rather than guessing a slot number.

data_description.name: Names the entity, e.g. "Alerts" gives notify.alerts.
data_description.recipients: Every message to this target goes to all of these.
data_description.device: Which gateway phone sends it. Leave empty for the gateway's own choice.
data_description.subject: Used when nothing passes a title with the message.

reconfigure.title: Edit notification target
reconfigure.description: Change what this target sends, and where it goes.

sim.title: Which SIM
sim.description:
Available SIMs: {sims}

Leave it empty to let the phone use its default SIM. A phone only reports its SIMs once it has been granted the phone permission, so an empty list means a slot number typed by hand is the only option.

data_description.sim_number: Used for calls as well as messages.
